engineGuide.h2: Nissan Terrano Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Nissan Terrano across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.7L Diesel
entries.history: Legendary gear-driven 2.7L turbodiesel, no timing belt or chain to fail - the most reliable Terrano engine.
compatibleVariants: WD21, R20, R50, 2.7 TD, 2.7 TDi, 2.7 TD27, 2.7 TD27TI, 4x4 Off-Road
commonFailures: Head gasket failure on long inclines (80k+ miles)
Cooling system corrosion in high-mileage units
Injector wear causing hard starting (100k+ miles)
entries.cta: Get quotes for TD27 Nissan Terrano engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L Diesel
entries.history: Intercooled evolution of the TD27, delivering 125hp with the same legendary gear-driven reliability.
compatibleVariants: R20, R50, 2.7 TD27TI, 4x4, Off-Road, Tow Pack
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil seal wear (90k+ miles)
EGR valve clogging on short-journey use
Intercooler pipe cracking under heavy load
entries.cta: Get quotes for TD27TI Nissan Terrano engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Common-rail 3.0L diesel introduced on R50, offering more power but with HPFP vulnerability after 100k miles.
compatibleVariants: R50, 3.0 dCi, 3.0 dCi Acenta, 3.0 dCi Tekna, Automatic
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump failure (OEM 17040-7F000) after 100k miles
EGR cooler cracking causing coolant loss
Turbo actuator sticking on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for ZD30DDTI Nissan Terrano engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: Smaller 2.5L common-rail option for R50, popular for fuel economy but prone to turbo and HPFP issues.
compatibleVariants: R50, 2.5 dCi, 2.5 dCi Acenta, 2.5 dCi Tekna
commonFailures: Turbocharger failure (OEM 14600-7F000) due to oil feed clogging
HPFP wear causing poor fuel pressure (100k+ miles)
DPF clogging on short-journey driving patterns
entries.cta: Get quotes for YD25DDT Nissan Terrano engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Renault-sourced 1.5L dCi used in Indian-market P20/P30 Terranos, timing chain tensioner issues per SB1-02A.
compatibleVariants: P20, P30, 1.5 dCi, XV, Premium, Grey Import
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (Nissan SB1-02A)
EGR cooler carbon buildup causing reduced power
DPF regeneration issues on urban use
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K Nissan Terrano engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: Larger 2.2L diesel option for late P30 models, sharing EGR and turbo vulnerabilities with K9K family.
compatibleVariants: P30, 2.2 dCi, XV Premium, Grey Import
commonFailures: EGR cooler failure across all variants
Turbocharger oil seal wear (80k+ miles)
Injector leak-off causing hard starting
entries.cta: Get quotes for ED12DTI Nissan Terrano engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Smooth 3.0L V6 petrol for early WD21 Terranos, timing chain tensioner failure at ~100k miles is the critical weakness.
compatibleVariants: WD21, R20, 3.0 V6, 3.0 SE, 3.0 SLX, 4x4 Petrol
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner plastic guide failure (~100k miles)
Chain slap wearing hole in timing cover
Oil pump wear causing low pressure on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for VG30E Nissan Terrano engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Economical 1.6L petrol for Indian-market P20/P30 Terranos, oil pump failures documented on high-mileage examples.
compatibleVariants: P20, P30, 1.6 Petrol, XV, Premium, Grey Import
commonFailures: Oil pump failure (OEM 13200-1J000) causing low pressure
Timing chain stretch on neglected service history
VVT solenoid clogging causing rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for HR16DE Nissan Terrano engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Nissan Terrano Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Nissan Terrano's engine reliability varies significantly by generation and fuel type. The legendary TD27 diesel is robust but prone to head gasket issues on long climbs, while the VG30E petrol suffers timing chain tensioner failure around 100k miles. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Terrano.

problems.h4: VG30E Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Petrol Terranos Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Terrano WD21/R20 3.0 V6 Petrol (1993-1999, VG30E)
problems.rootCause: Plastic timing chain tensioner guide breaks, allowing chain slap that can wear a hole in the timing cover.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace plastic tensioner guide and chain before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before cover damage; not suitable if hole already worn

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Repair or replace damaged timing cover, fit upgraded tensioner components
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; underlying chain wear may cause repeat failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioners, new head gasket, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; 12-month warranty covers timing components

problems.recommendation: On a WD21 Terrano worth £2,000-£4,000, preventative tensioner replacement at 80k miles offers excellent value. If the timing cover is already damaged, a rebuilt VG30E with upgraded components is more cost-effective than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt VG30E engine prices for your Nissan Terrano

problems.h4: TD27 Overheating & Head Gasket Failure - Why Diesel Terranos Run Hot
problems.rootCause: Cylinder head cracking or head gasket failure under sustained load, often exacerbated by cooling system neglect.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace radiator, thermostat, hoses, pressure test system
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative only; not suitable if head is already cracked

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, machine head if warped
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is undamaged; risk of repeat failure on high-mileage engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt TD27 with new cylinder head, upgraded cooling components
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value; gear-driven timing eliminates belt/chain risks

problems.recommendation: The TD27's legendary reliability makes it worth rebuilding. If overheating symptoms appear, act immediately - a rebuilt unit with new head and upgraded cooling is more economical than repeated gasket replacements on a 150k-mile engine.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt TD27 Nissan Terrano engine replacement

problems.h4: ZD30/YD25 HPFP Failure - Why R50 Diesel Terranos Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Terrano R50 3.0 dCi / 2.5 dCi (2000-2006, ZD30DDTI/YD25DDT)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure fuel pump (OEM 17040-7F000) internal wear causing low fuel pressure, poor starting, and power loss.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - HPFP Filter & Fuel System Clean
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace fuel filters, clean injectors, test pump pressure
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary measure; does not address internal pump wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned HPFP, calibrate injection timing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if injectors and rails are undamaged; pump may fail again

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded HPFP, new injectors, EGR components
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution; addresses all common R50 diesel failure points

problems.recommendation: HPFP failure on R50 diesels is often symptomatic of broader fuel system wear. A rebuilt engine with upgraded pump and injectors offers better long-term value than repeated pump replacements, especially on vehicles over 100k miles.
problems.cta: Compare ZD30DDTI rebuilt engine prices for your Nissan Terrano R50

problems.h4: K9K Timing Chain Tensioner Issues - Why Indian-Market Terranos Rattle
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner design flaw addressed in Nissan Service Bulletin SB1-02A; plastic components wear prematurely.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner and plastic guides per SB1-02A update
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative; only viable if chain hasn't stretched or jumped

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides, and sprockets
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine hasn't suffered valve damage from timing jump

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K9K with upgraded tensioner components, new head gasket
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if chain has jumped or valve damage suspected

problems.recommendation: Grey import P20/P30 Terranos with K9K engines benefit from early tensioner replacement at 60k miles. If rattling is already present, a rebuilt engine with SB1-02A-compliant components is safer than risking valve damage from chain jump.
problems.cta: Enquire about K9K rebuilt engine availability for your Nissan Terrano P20/P30

problems.h4: YD25DDT Turbocharger Failure - Why 2.5 dCi Terranos Smoke
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger oil feed clogging (OEM 14600-7F000) causing oil starvation, bearing wear, and blue smoke.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Oil Feed Clean & Seal Replace
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean oil feed line, replace seals, test boost pressure
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary; does not address bearing wear inside turbo

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned turbo, replace oil feed line, calibrate actuator
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine internals are undamaged; risk of repeat failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo, new oil feed components
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution; addresses turbo, HPFP, and EGR failure points

problems.recommendation: Turbo failure on YD25 engines often indicates broader oil system contamination. A rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo and cleaned oil galleries offers better reliability than turbo-only replacement on high-mileage units.
problems.cta: Compare YD25DDT rebuilt engine prices for your Nissan Terrano R50

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan Terrano
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Terrano suffers VG30E timing chain rattle, TD27 overheating, or ZD30 HPFP failure, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Avoid main dealer premiums and get back on the road with confidence.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan Terrano Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan Terrano and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Nissan Terrano Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Nissan Terrano across UK generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Nissan Terrano 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The smallest diesel option, the 1.5L K9K was fitted to Indian-market P20/P30 Terranos sold as grey imports in the UK from 2013, offering modern common-rail efficiency but with timing chain tensioner vulnerabilities addressed in service bulletin SB1-02A.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner failure per SB1-02A
EGR cooler clogging on urban use
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Terrano 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan Terrano 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2L ED12DTI appeared on late P30 grey imports as a larger diesel option, sharing the K9K family's EGR and turbo vulnerabilities while offering improved torque for towing and off-road use.
commonFailurePoints: EGR cooler failure across all variants
turbo oil seal wear at high mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Terrano 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan Terrano 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.5L YD25DDT was introduced on R50 Terranos from 2002 as a fuel-efficient alternative to the 3.0L, but turbocharger and HPFP failures after 100k miles drive significant replacement demand among UK owners.
commonFailurePoints: HPFP wear causing poor fuel pressure
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Terrano 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan Terrano 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The legendary 2.7L TD27 series powered original WD21 and early R50 Terranos, featuring gear-driven timing with no belt or chain to fail - making it the most sought-after replacement engine for UK 4x4 enthusiasts and overlanders.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure on sustained inclines
cooling system corrosion on high-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Terrano 2.7L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan Terrano 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L ZD30DDTI replaced the TD27 on R50 Terranos from 2000, offering common-rail refinement and 154hp but with HPFP vulnerability after 100k miles that drives replacement enquiries among UK owners.
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Terrano 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan Terrano 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L HR16DE was fitted to Indian-market P20/P30 Terranos sold as grey imports, offering economical petrol power but with documented oil pump failures on high-mileage examples requiring careful supplier selection.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain stretch on neglected service history

items.title: Nissan Terrano 3.0L Petrol Engines

commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner plastic guide failure (~100k miles)

fuelTypes.h2: Nissan Terrano Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Nissan Terrano engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the legendary 2.7L TD27 diesel to the Indian-market 1.6L HR16DE petrol.

items.title: Nissan Terrano Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel engines dominate UK Terrano replacement demand, with the legendary 2.7L TD27 series offering unmatched reliability and the later ZD30/YD25 common-rail units requiring careful supplier selection due to HPFP vulnerabilities.
items.descriptor: Diesel engines dominate UK Terrano replacement demand, with the legendary 2.7L TD27 series offering unmatched reliability and the later ZD30/YD25 common-rail units requiring careful supplier selection due to HPFP vulnerabilities.
items.familiesLabel: Common Nissan Terrano Diesel Engine Families
families: TD27/TD27TI - most sought-after replacement engine (WD21/R50), gear-driven timing eliminates belt/chain risks
ZD30DDTI - common-rail 3.0L (R50), HPFP failure after 100k miles drives replacement demand
YD25DDT - fuel-efficient 2.5L (R50), turbocharger and HPFP issues on high-mileage examples
K9K - Renault-sourced 1.5L (P20/P30 grey imports), timing chain tensioner issues per SB1-02A
knownFor: TD27's legendary reliability and gear-driven timing design
ZD30/YD25 HPFP failures requiring specialist rebuilt units
K9K timing chain tensioner issues addressed in service bulletins
Strong off-road torque across all diesel variants

items.title: Nissan Terrano Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Terranos are less common in the UK but the VG30E V6 offers smooth performance if timing chain tensioner issues are addressed preventatively, while grey import 1.6L HR16DE units require careful oil pump inspection.
items.descriptor: Petrol Terranos are less common in the UK but the VG30E V6 offers smooth performance if timing chain tensioner issues are addressed preventatively, while grey import 1.6L HR16DE units require careful oil pump inspection.
items.familiesLabel: Common Nissan Terrano Petrol Engine Families
families: VG30E - smooth 3.0L V6 (WD21), timing chain tensioner failure at ~100k miles is critical weakness
HR16DE - economical 1.6L (P20/P30 grey imports), oil pump failures documented on high-mileage examples
knownFor: VG30E's smooth power delivery but critical timing cover vulnerability
HR16DE's fuel economy but oil pump reliability concerns

Petrol variants often preferred for lighter towing applications

items.description: No hybrid or electrified variants of the Nissan Terrano were officially sold in the UK market; all replacement enquiries relate to conventional diesel or petrol engines.

knownFor: TD27's legendary reliability and gear-driven timing design

faq.h2: Nissan Terrano Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Nissan Terrano engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Nissan Terrano engine replacement costs vary by engine type and condition. Used TD27 engines start from £1,200, while rebuilt units with upgraded components range from £2,800-£3,200 including 12-month warranty.
keyPoints: TD27 rebuilt engines: £2,800-£3,200 supply only
VG30E petrol rebuilt: £2,000-£2,500 with upgraded tensioners
ZD30DDTI rebuilt: £3,000-£3,500 with HPFP upgrade
Labour for supply & fit typically adds £400-£700
Prices include bench testing and 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare Nissan Terrano engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the Nissan Terrano TD27 engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes, the TD27 is widely regarded as one of the most reliable diesel engines in its category, featuring gear-driven timing with no belt or chain to fail, though head gasket issues can occur on sustained inclines.
keyPoints: Gear-driven timing eliminates belt/chain failure risks
Head gasket vulnerability on long climbs at high mileage
Runs reliably on lower-quality diesel and biodiesel blends
Strong aftermarket support from UK 4x4 specialists
Preferred engine for overlanding and heavy towing applications
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt TD27 Nissan Terrano engines with upgraded cooling

items.question: What causes Nissan Terrano VG30E timing chain failure?
items.answer: The VG30E petrol engine suffers timing chain tensioner failure around 94k-100k miles when the plastic guide breaks, allowing chain slap that can wear a hole in the timing cover.
keyPoints: Plastic tensioner guide is the critical weak component
Failure typically occurs between 94,000-100,000 miles
Chain slap can wear hole in aluminium timing cover
Rebuilt engines include upgraded metal tensioner components
Preventative replacement at 80k miles avoids catastrophic damage
items.cta: Compare rebuilt VG30E engines with upgraded timing components for your Terrano

items.question: Are Ford Maverick engines compatible with Nissan Terrano?
items.answer: Yes, the Nissan Terrano WD21 and Ford Maverick (1993-1998) are mechanically identical rebadged vehicles, sharing TD27, VG30E and ZD30 engines with direct parts compatibility.
keyPoints: WD21 Terrano and Maverick share identical chassis and powertrains
TD27, VG30E and ZD30 engines are direct swaps between models
Maverick breakers expand UK supplier options for Terrano parts
ECU and wiring harness compatibility confirmed for engine swaps
Specialist knowledge recommended for cross-model installations
items.cta: Find Ford Maverick compatible engines for your Nissan Terrano WD21

items.question: What is the difference between original and Indian-market Nissan Terrano?
items.answer: Original UK Terrano (WD21/R50, 1993-2006) uses TD27/VG30E/ZD30 engines with zero parts interchangeability with Indian-market P20/P30 (2013-2019) which use K9K/HR16DE engines.
keyPoints: Indian-market models: P20 (2013-2016), P30 (2016-2019) grey imports
Engine families completely different with no cross-compatibility
Indian models sold only as grey imports in UK market
Verify generation via registration check before ordering replacement
items.cta: Verify your Nissan Terrano generation before ordering engine replacement

items.question: Does the Nissan Terrano TD27 have a timing belt or chain?
items.answer: The TD27 uses gear-driven timing with neither belt nor chain, making it one of the most reliable designs with no timing component to snap or stretch.
keyPoints: Gear-driven timing eliminates belt/chain failure risks entirely
No scheduled timing component replacement required
Major contributor to TD27's legendary reliability reputation
Only maintenance needed is regular oil changes and cooling system care
Key selling point for rebuilt TD27 units from UK specialists
items.cta: Get quotes for gear-driven TD27 Nissan Terrano engine replacement

items.question: How long does a Nissan Terrano engine replacement take?
items.answer: TD27/TD27TI replacements typically take 2-3 days due to robust gear-driven design, while VG30E petrol swaps may complete in 1-2 days with specialist 4x4 fitters.
keyPoints: TD27 gear-driven design simplifies removal and fitting process
Specialist 4x4 garages preferred for correct transfer case handling
Supply & fit packages include ECU adaptation and road testing
Next-day engine delivery available on TD27 units from UK stock
Allow extra time for grey import P20/P30 engine sourcing
items.cta: Find Nissan Terrano engine supply & fit specialists nationwide

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Nissan Terrano engine?
items.answer: Yes, for original WD21/R50 Terranos valued £2,000-£6,000, a rebuilt TD27 from £2,800 offers excellent value given the model's off-road capability and enthusiast demand.
keyPoints: Original 4x4 Terranos retain strong enthusiast values in UK market
TD27 rebuilt cost represents good value vs vehicle worth
Rebuilt engines include warranty and upgraded failure-point components
Grey import P20/P30 valuations lower; assess cost vs benefit carefully
Consider vehicle condition, mileage and intended use before deciding
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Nissan Terrano engine options for your budget

items.question: What warranty comes with Nissan Terrano replacement engines?
items.answer: Used engines typically include 3-6 month warranty, while rebuilt/reconditioned units from EngineMarket suppliers carry minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering known failure points.
keyPoints: Used engines: 3-6 month warranty, check for head cracks on TD27
Rebuilt engines: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers timing components, HPFP, EGR cooler on relevant engines
Always confirm exact terms with chosen supplier before ordering
EngineMarket network vets suppliers for warranty claim reliability
